member.name: T:SeaShell.Sea
member.summary:
Runtime context for SeaShell scripts. Available in every script via `Sea.*`.
Provides script identity, environment info, build metadata, and hot-swap lifecycle events.

member.name: P:SeaShell.Sea.ScriptPath
member.summary: Absolute path to the main script file.

member.name: P:SeaShell.Sea.ScriptDir
member.summary: Directory containing the main script.

member.name: P:SeaShell.Sea.ScriptName
member.summary: Script filename without extension.

member.name: P:SeaShell.Sea.ScriptFile
member.summary: Script filename with extension.

member.name: P:SeaShell.Sea.StartDir
member.summary: The directory where 'sea' was invoked. Always correct — SeaShell never changes CWD, unlike CS-Script which switches to the script's directory.

member.name: P:SeaShell.Sea.IsElevated
member.summary: Whether the script is running with elevated privileges (admin/root).

member.name: P:SeaShell.Sea.IsConsoleEphemeral
member.summary: True when sea.exe detected the console window will likely close on exit (e.g., script was double-clicked from Explorer rather than run from a terminal). Always false when running via SeaShell.Host.

member.name: P:SeaShell.Sea.ExitDelay
member.summary: Seconds to delay before the console window closes when is true. The CLI shows an interactive countdown that the user can skip (Enter), cancel (Escape), or adjust (arrow keys). Set to 0 to exit immediately. Default: 7 seconds. Ignored when not ephemeral or when running via Host.

member.name: P:SeaShell.Sea.Args
member.summary: Script arguments (same as top-level 'args' but explicitly named).

member.name: P:SeaShell.Sea.Sources
member.summary: All source files that were compiled (main script + includes), in dependency order.

member.name: P:SeaShell.Sea.Packages
member.summary: NuGet packages resolved for this script (name → version).

member.name: P:SeaShell.Sea.Assemblies
member.summary: Managed assembly paths loaded for this script.

member.name: E:SeaShell.Sea.Reloading
member.summary: Fires when the CLI is about to replace this process with a recompiled version. Use this to save state, close connections, flush logs, etc. Call during this event to pass state to the new instance. After this event completes (or the grace period expires), the process is killed.

member.name: E:SeaShell.Sea.Stopping
member.summary: Fires when the CLI requests a clean stop (e.g., Ctrl+C, `sea --stop`).

member.name: E:SeaShell.Sea.MessageReceived
member.summary: Fires when the Host sends an application message during execution. Parameters: payload (raw bytes), topic (optional routing key). Only fires when running via ScriptHost, not CLI.

member.name: P:SeaShell.Sea.IsWatchMode
member.summary: True when running in watch mode (//sea_watch). Enables file-change hot-swap.

member.name: P:SeaShell.Sea.IsReload
member.summary: True if this instance was started as a hot-swap replacement (not the first run).

member.name: P:SeaShell.Sea.ReloadCount
member.summary: How many times this script has been reloaded (0 on first run).

member.name: P:SeaShell.Sea.IsShuttingDown
member.summary: True after Reloading or Stopping has fired. Check this in loops.

member.name: P:SeaShell.Sea.ShutdownToken
member.summary: A cancellation token that is cancelled when Reloading or Stopping fires. Pass this to async operations for automatic cancellation on reload.

member.name: M:SeaShell.Sea.SetReloadState(System.Byte[])
member.summary: Pass state to the next instance during a reload. Call this from the Reloading event. The byte array is passed to the new process and available via GetReloadState(). Maximum 8 KB.

member.name: M:SeaShell.Sea.SetReloadState(System.String)
member.summary: Pass a string state to the next instance during a reload. Convenience wrapper around SetReloadState(byte[]).

member.name: M:SeaShell.Sea.GetReloadState
member.summary: Retrieve state passed from the previous instance via SetReloadState(). Returns null on first run or if no state was passed.

member.name: M:SeaShell.Sea.GetReloadStateString
member.summary: Retrieve string state passed from the previous instance. Returns null on first run or if no state was passed.

member.name: M:SeaShell.Sea.SendMessageAsync(System.Byte[],System.String)
member.summary: Send a binary message to the Host. No-op if not connected.

member.name: M:SeaShell.Sea.SendMessageAsync(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Send a string message to the Host (UTF-8 encoded). No-op if not connected.

member.name: M:SeaShell.Sea.SendMessage(System.Byte[],System.String)
member.summary: Send a binary message to the Host (synchronous). No-op if not connected.

member.name: M:SeaShell.Sea.SendMessage(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Send a string message to the Host (synchronous, UTF-8). No-op if not connected.

member.name: M:SeaShell.Sea.RequestReload(System.Boolean,System.String)
member.summary: Request this script to be recompiled and reloaded. Triggers the same hot-swap path as a file change. Works in both direct and watch mode.
member.param: If true, forces a full recompile by clearing the compilation cache.

member.name: M:SeaShell.Sea.RequestReloadAsync(System.Boolean,System.String)
member.summary: Async version of .

member.name: M:SeaShell.Sea.EnsureInitialized
member.summary: Called by the script assembly's module initializer (_SeaShellBoot) to guarantee initialization runs before any script code, regardless of whether the script references Sea.* types directly.

member.name: T:SeaShell.StartupHook
member.summary: Required by DOTNET_STARTUP_HOOKS. When SeaShell.Script.dll is specified as a startup hook, the .NET runtime calls this before the application's Main(). This gives pre-compiled binaries that don't reference SeaShell.Script full Sea context (IPC channel, lifecycle events, etc.).
